records.ety: [OE. letuaire, OF. lettuaire, electuaire, F. lectuaire, L. electuarium, electarium. prob. fr. Gr. <?/, <?/ a medicine that is licked away, fr. Gr. <?/ to lick up; 'ek out + <?/ to lick. See Lick, and cf. Eclegm.]

defs: A medicine composed of powders, or other ingredients, incorporated with some conserver, honey, or sirup; a confection. See the note under Confection.
